text: Large language models (LLMs) are advanced computer programs that understand and generate human-like text. They work by processing vast amounts of written data from books, articles, and websites, learning patterns in language to predict the next word in a sentence. For training, these models are fed enormous datasets, which helps them grasp grammar, facts, and even some reasoning. Users interact with LLMs through prompt engineering—crafting specific questions or instructions to get desired outputs, like writing a summary or answering questions. Their capabilities include translating languages, creating content, and assisting with learning. However, they have limits: they can produce incorrect information, lack real understanding, and sometimes reflect biases in their training data, so careful use is essential.
